macro_segments.title: Tumah of the Dead and Entry to the Beit Hamikdash

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Eliezer's Teaching on Tumah of the Dead and Obligation to Avoid the Beit Hamikdash
Rabbi Meir's Challenge: Comparison to Sheretz
Beraitah: Rabbi Yehoshua ben Peser Rosh's Teaching to Rabbi Meir
Rabbi Eliezer's Response: Tradition from Rabbi Yehoshua ben Mamo

macro_segments.title: Transmission of Tradition and the Omission of Intermediate Steps

micro_segments.title: The Principle of Stating First and Last Rather Than All Intermediate Authorities
Example from Nachman haLavlur: Tradition Chain from the Zugot
Question of Why Yehoshua and Chalev Are Omitted from the Chain

macro_segments.title: Rabbi Akiva's Logical Argument on Blood and Bone Regarding Nazir Shaving

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Akiva's Kal Vachomer: Bone Size and Quarter-Log of Blood
Rabbi Eliezer's Dismissal: Svaras Have No Place Here
Rabbi Yehoshua's Validation: Good Argument But It's Halakha

macro_segments.title: Gemara Analysis: Which Is the Halakha L'Moshe M'Sinai?

micro_segments.title: The Question: Is the Bone Exception or the Blood Measurement the Halakha?
Resolution: Bone Is the Halakha, Cannot Derive Kal Vachomer from It
Principle: Cannot Make Kal Vachomer from a Case Designated as Halakha L'Moshe M'Sinai

macro_segments.title: Two Nazirims with Uncertain Tumah Status

micro_segments.title: Scenario: One Person Saw One of Two Nazirims Become Tamei But Cannot Identify Which
Practical Resolution: Both Shave and Bring Korbanot with Stipulation

macro_segments.title: Two Nazirims with Uncertain Tumah Status (continued)

micro_segments.title: Two Thirty-Day Periods: First for Potential Tahara, Second for Potential Tumah

macro_segments.title: Gemara Questions on Safek Tumah in Private vs. Public Domain

micro_segments.title: Principle from Sotah: Safek Tumah in Private Domain Is Tamei, in Public Is Tahor
Three People Present: Witness Plus Two Nazirims Makes Public Domain
Alternative Explanation: Witness Stood at Distance, Making Nazirims' Space Private

macro_segments.title: The Problem of Preventing Transgression When One Shaver May Not Be Required

micro_segments.title: Challenge: Why Can One Who Is Tahor Cut His Hair Twice, Committing Hakafa?
Shmuel's Answer: Case of Woman or Minor Not Subject to Prohibition
Gemara's Alternative: Perhaps Shaving Entire Head Is Not Hakafa
Inference: Shmuel Holds That Shaving All Hair IS Hakafa and Prohibited

macro_segments.title: Parallel Case: Four Shavings for Safek Metzora and Safek Tamei Nazir

micro_segments.title: Mishnah: Nazir with Safek Tumah and Safek Tzaraat Performs Four Shavings Over Sixty Days
Same Question and Application of Shmuel's Answer About Women and Minors

macro_segments.title: Rav Huna's Position: The Shaver and Shavee Are Independent

micro_segments.title: Rav Huna: An Adult Male Shaver Transgresses Regardless of Whom He Shaves
Rav Adda's Challenge: How Does Rav Huna's Wife Shave His Children?
Rav Huna's Answer: A Woman Can Shave Because She Is Exempt as a Shaver
Conclusion: Only Adult Males Cannot Be in Either Role; Women and Minors Can Be Either
Historical Note: Rav Adda's Curse and Its Effect on Rav Huna's Progeny
